160	triage is separate and runs only after evidence exists. The hook has enough
161	context to evaluate title, description, acceptance criteria, labels, parent,
162	deps, bead type, spec-id, prior attempt history, and whether the bead is
163	atomic enough to execute. The hook invokes the nested bead-lifecycle workflow
164	skill under the `ddx` skill tree and records readiness evidence in the
165	layer-3 run record.
166	
167	`ddx work` wires this hook by default in both CLI and server-managed worker
168	paths. Decomposition decisions run with a strong `MinPower` floor, defaulting to
169	the smart/top-power tier floor when no project-specific splitter override is
170	configured. DDx does not choose the concrete model; it passes the raised power
171	floor to Fizeau and preserves any operator-supplied harness/provider/model
172	passthrough constraints. If those constraints cannot satisfy the strong floor,
173	DDx records `agent_power_unsatisfied` and blocks instead of running weak
174	decomposition.
175	
176	The readiness assessment result is one of:
177	
178	- `actionable_atomic` — claim and execute normally.
179	- `actionable_but_rewritten` — DDx applied a validated replacement rewrite or
180	  metadata update that makes the bead more execution-ready before claim. Safe
181	  rewrites may replace noisy, stale, duplicated, or brittle description text
182	  with a fit-for-purpose bead body instead of appending more prompt burden. They may
183	  also format the description into the authoring template, add discovered
184	  file:line evidence, add an obvious test command, or wire deterministic
185	  labels/parent/deps. The mutation is recorded through `ddx bead` paths before
186	  claim, with before/after hashes and enough evidence to audit the replacement.
187	- `too_large_decomposed` — DDx created child beads, mapped every parent AC to
188	  child ACs or an explicit `needs_human` / `non_scope` marker, blocked the
189	  parent, and did not execute the parent.
190	- `ambiguous_needs_human` — the bead/spec is unclear, contradictory,
191	  unverifiable, or missing acceptance criteria that DDx cannot safely invent.
192	  DDx blocks or marks the bead `needs_human` and does not claim it.
193	- `readiness_error` — readiness infrastructure failed. In migration/WARN mode
194	  this fails open with evidence; in reliable factory/BLOCK mode it may skip the
195	  candidate for the current pass but must not park it behind a cooldown unless a

121	### Fail-Open Behavior
122	
123	The lifecycle hooks follow reliability principle P1's fail-open posture for
124	infrastructure failures.
125	
126	- If the hook cannot run, the workflow skill is missing, output cannot be
127	  parsed, or evidence cannot be written, DDx records the infrastructure failure
128	  and proceeds.
129	- In WARN-ONLY mode, all valid lint results proceed after reporting.
130	- In BLOCK mode, only a valid post-waiver lint result below the configured
131	  threshold blocks dispatch.
132	- Post-attempt triage failures never erase the attempt result. They annotate the
133	  report and evidence so retry policy and operators can distinguish "agent made
134	  a bad attempt" from "quality infrastructure failed."
135	
136	### Safe Improvement, Decomposition, And Recovery UX
137	
138	Bead readiness assessment may update a bead before execution only when the
139	update is intent-preserving and grounded in durable context. Safe improvements
140	include validated replacement rewrites of the description, normalizing the bead
141	body into the authoring template, adding discovered file:line evidence, adding
142	an obvious subsystem test command, and wiring deterministic labels, parent, or
143	dependencies.
144	
145	Validated replacement is preferred over append-only amendment when replacement
146	would make the bead a better implementation prompt. Prompt fitness is part of
147	the quality policy: readiness should leave the next agent enough durable context
148	to execute without dragging along stale, duplicated, or chat-shaped noise. A
149	one-line vague bead may need to expand into a standalone task; a noisy bead may
150	need to shrink. The original bead text remains auditable through the readiness
151	event and before/after hashes; it does not have to be embedded verbatim in the
152	replacement prompt body.
153	
154	The validator proves preservation from durable anchors rather than asking
155	whether the new bead is merely "in the spirit" of the old one. Acceptance
156	criteria, non-scope, governing artifact references, dependencies, named
157	files/tests, and still-valid root-cause evidence are explicit commitments and
158	must be preserved or replaced with current durable evidence. If the old bead
159	contains contradictions, stale anchors, or missing product choices, readiness may
160	remove the confusing wording only when the replacement clearly marks the
161	unresolved part as `needs_human` or preserves it as an explicit constraint.
162	Readiness must block for human input instead of inventing acceptance criteria,
163	changing scope, choosing between conflicting requirements, deleting unresolved
164	constraints, or guessing a missing governing artifact.
165	
166	If readiness finds the bead too broad, it decomposes before claim. Every parent AC
167	must map to at least one child AC or be explicitly marked `needs_human` or
168	`non_scope`; token-overlap metrics are heuristics, not proof of preservation.
169	The parent is blocked/decomposed with child ids and the AC map in evidence.
170	
171	When BLOCK mode stops dispatch, the operator-facing output must be actionable.
172	It prints:
173	
174	- bead id, score, mode, and blocked criteria
175	- the missing fields or malformed sections in plain language
176	- the waiver labels already applied and the labels that would waive each
177	  failed criterion
178	- suggested `ddx bead update` commands for adding description, acceptance,
179	  labels, parent, notes, or dependency metadata
180	- the `--force --reason` form for exceptional dispatch, with the note that an
181	  evidence event will be recorded
182	
183	Recovery output must not require reading the lint implementation or an
184	out-of-band report to fix an ordinary authoring problem.
185	
186	## Consequences
187	
188	- FEAT-004 owns the label-based waiver storage and the no-new-schema rule.
189	- FEAT-010 owns hook insertion in `ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime`, attempt
190	  classification, and the `OutcomeReason` report field.
191	- FEAT-011 owns nested workflow-skill packaging so the same rubric guidance can
192	  be reused by lint, triage, review, breakdown, replay, and benchmark flows.
193	- Legacy beads can continue running in WARN-ONLY while the queue is baselined.
194	- Once BLOCK mode is enabled, dispatch quality becomes explicit and auditable
195	  instead of depending on operator memory.
196	
197	## Non-Goals

**Changes committed in `feat(agent): replace append-only intake check with validated replacement policy [ddx-a41e6fbb]`** (the code was in the prior checkpoint):

Extended `preClaimIntakeRewriteEventBody` with `PreservationEvidence []string`. Updated `validateAndApplyPreClaimIntakeRewrite` signature to return preservation evidence.
